title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2026-A002: Prepaid Card Structuring and Reload Aggregation Schemes
content: FinCEN is issuing this advisory to alert covered financial institutions and Money Services Businesses to a structuring methodology observed in 2024-2025 examinations involving open-loop prepaid access products. Subjects circumvent Currency Transaction Report (31 CFR 1010.314) requirements by acquiring multiple prepaid cards at retail locations, each below the CTR threshold, and subsequently reloading the same cards through Money Services Business agents. In a representative case the subject acquired 11 prepaid cards in a single day across three retailers in Phoenix, AZ, with initial loads ranging from USD 1,800 to USD 4,900 per card. Over the following six business days, cumulative reloads on the same cards totaled USD 84,750, with each individual reload sized between USD 950 and USD 4,750. The aggregate behavior is functionally identical to traditional cash structuring (31 USC 5324) but evades detection at single-institution monitoring because reload transactions are distributed across MSB agents. FinCEN reminds institutions that the existing structuring typology covers this conduct; institutions should ensure their transaction monitoring scenarios incorporate prepaid card reload activity into structuring detection logic.

extracted_typologies.name: Structuring / Smurfing
extracted_typologies.definition: Structuring is the deliberate breaking up of a cash transaction into smaller amounts below the $10,000 CTR threshold to evade Bank Secrecy Act reporting. FinCEN Advisory FIN-2026-A002 confirms that the same evasive intent and underlying conduct extends to open-loop prepaid card acquisitions and subsequent reloads through MSB agents, and that institutions must cover this variant within their existing structuring monitoring scenarios.
extracted_typologies.example: A subject acquires 11 prepaid cards across three retailers in a single day with initial loads sized between $1,800 and $4,900 per card (none above the CTR threshold), then over the following six business days reloads the same cards through MSB agents in increments of $950 to $4,750, accumulating $84,750 of additional value. No individual transaction triggers a CTR; the aggregate behavior is the same cash structuring pattern executed across the prepaid channel.
